{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
