{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/kotozuka.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Kotozuka Kofun

It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/kotozuka.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} Kotozuka Kofun [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kotozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kotozuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 5th century]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kotozuka Kofun]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kotozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]]
}} [[sn:Kotozuka Kofun]] [[en:Kotozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gifu, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[Gifu]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[Chūbu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/kotozuka.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} Kotozuka Kofun

It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|05|N|136|48|52|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/kotozuka.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} Kotozuka Kofun

''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11573757]] Wikidata name: Kotozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.418186111111|N|136.81474166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798}} | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kotozuka Kofun]][[en:Kotozuka Kofun]][[ja:琴塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11573757]] Wikidata name: Kotozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.418186111111|N|136.81474166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798}} | region = [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[:en:Gifu|Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Chūbu region|Chūbu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] of the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.418186111111|N|136.81474166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.418186111111|N|136.81474166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/kotozuka.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} Kotozuka Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | name = Kotozuka Kofun | native_name =琴塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kotozuka-Kofun CCB1987-1C7B19.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kotozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.418186111111|N|136.81474166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kotozuka Kofun'''|琴塚古墳||}} is a''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Kotozuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190822|title=琴塚古墳|trans-title=Kotozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is the third largest in Gifu Prefecture.
==Overview== The Kotozuka Kofun is a ''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}'' (前方後円墳), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|qid=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} near the border of the city of Gifu with the city of [[:en:Kakamigahara, Gifu|Kakamigahara]]. It is the only survivor of the Kotozuka cluster of ''kofun'', which once had many keyhole-shaped tumuli, all of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with a total length of 115 meters. The posterior circular portion is 69 meters in diameter, with a height of 10.5 meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 74.4 meters and a height of 7.5 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a double moat. The inner moat is mostly intact and has a width of 18 to 20 meters. The outer moat has largely been filled in, but surviving portions in the northwest and east have a width of 7.2 meters, with 10.5 meters separating the moats.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
''[[Fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the outside of the tumulus, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, stone spearheads and arrowheads. These artifacts and the construction style of tumulus dates the site to the 5th century AD. According to local legend, the tumulus is the tomb of the concubine of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{nihongo|Igoto-hime-no-mikoto|五十琴姫命}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|qid=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
